articles.title: Site arrival checklist
articles.summary: A quick checklist to prevent rework and speed up ticket closeout.
content: Confirm the ticket number and device ID before starting.
Badge in and record time-in.
Capture LED states and visible errors.
Take label photos before making changes.
Verify device power and cabling before escalating.

articles.title: Safe device reboot procedure
articles.summary: Steps for rebooting network devices without disrupting active users.
content: Notify any impacted users if the device is shared.
Check device logs for critical errors before rebooting.
Perform a soft reboot using the management interface if available.
Wait for link lights and status indicators to stabilize.
Verify connectivity after the device returns online.

articles.title: Basic network triage
articles.summary: Interactive troubleshooting flow for connectivity issues.

no_link.result: Check cable connections, confirm switch port status, and verify device power.

ip.question: Does the device receive an IP address?

dhcp.question: Is the switch port configured for the correct VLAN?

fix_vlan.result: Correct the VLAN assignment on the switch port and retry DHCP.

dhcp_scope.question: Is the DHCP scope healthy and available?

fix_scope.result: Restore or expand the DHCP scope before reconnecting the device.

escalate_dhcp.result: Escalate to the network team with VLAN ID, port number, and DHCP logs.

gateway.question: Can the device reach the default gateway?

gateway_issue.result: Investigate routing configuration or escalate with ping test results.

dns_issue.result: Verify DNS server configuration and connectivity.

service.question: Can the device reach the required service?

routing_issue.result: Investigate routing or firewall rules blocking service access.

app_layer.result: Network path appears healthy. Continue troubleshooting the application layer.

articles.title: Check Wi-Fi signal strength
articles.summary: Quick checks for diagnosing poor wireless performance.
content: Confirm the access point is powered and broadcasting the expected SSID.
Check signal strength using a Wi-Fi analyzer tool.
Verify the client device is connected to the correct network.
Look for sources of interference such as microwaves or dense metal shelving.
Test connectivity by pinging the gateway and external services.

articles.title: Device not powering on
articles.summary: Steps to diagnose a device that will not start or shows no status lights.
content: Confirm the power cable is fully inserted.
Test the outlet with another device.
Inspect the power adapter for damage.
Verify the device power switch is on.
If still unresponsive, escalate with device model and serial number.

articles.title: Common LED status indicators
articles.summary: Interpret common device LED patterns used during troubleshooting.
content: Solid green typically indicates normal operation.
Blinking amber often signals configuration or connectivity issues.
No light usually indicates power or hardware failure.
Rapid blinking may indicate firmware updates or boot cycles.
